title: People living in countries where a third gender is recognized
subtitle: Another gender alongside "man" and "woman" is recognized by law.
caption:
People living in countries where a third gender is recognized

Another gender alongside "man" and "woman" is recognized by law.

This stacked area chart tracks the global population distribution based on legal recognition of a third gender from 1991 to 2019. The vertical axis measures population in billions, ranging from 0 to 8 billion, while the horizontal axis marks years in four-year intervals. The chart is divided into two distinct colored regions. The upper, larger region, shaded in yellow-orange, represents the population living in countries where a third gender is not recognized. This area begins at approximately 5.4 billion in 1991 and expands steadily to roughly 7.7 billion by 2019, occupying the majority of the chart's vertical space throughout the timeline. The lower region, shaded in purple, indicates the population living in countries where a third gender is recognized. This section remains at zero from 1991 until approximately 2008. A sharp vertical increase occurs around 2009, where the purple area jumps from the baseline to approximately 1.4 billion. Following this rise, the purple area shows a gradual upward trend, reaching just under 2 billion by 2019. The boundary between the two regions clearly delineates the shift in population counts associated with changes in legal recognition status over the observed period.
